contentBlocks.original:
कदाचित् कालिन्दी तट विपिन सङ्गीत तरलो
मुदाभीरी नारी वदन कमला स्वाद मधुपः
रमा शम्भु ब्रह्मामरपति गणेशार्चित पदो
जगन्नाथः स्वामी नयन पथ गामी भवतु मे ॥१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kadācit kālindī taṭa vipina saṅgīta taralo
mudābhīrī nārī vadana kamalā svāda madhupaḥ
ramā śambhu brahmāmarapati gaṇeśārcita pado
jagannāthaḥ svāmī nayana patha gāmī bhavatu me ..1..
contentBlocks.translation: Sometimes in great happiness Lord Jagannatha, with his flute, makes a loud concert in the groves on the banks of the Yamuna. He is like a bumblebee who tastes the beautiful lotus-like faces of the cowherd damsels of Vraja, and his lotus feet are worshiped by Lakshmi, Siva, Brahma, Indra and Ganesa. May that lord be the object of my vision.
contentBlocks.meanings: कदाचित् (kadācit) – sometimes; कालिन्दी (kālindī) – Yamuna; तट (taṭa) – bank; विपिन (vipina) – grove; सङ्गीत (saṅgīta) – concert; तरलः (taralaḥ) – agile; मुदा (mudā) – with joy; अभिरी (abhīrī) – cowherd damsels; नारी (nārī) – woman; वदन (vadana) – face; कमला (kamalā) – lotus; स्वाद (svāda) – taste; मधुपः (madhu-paḥ) – bumblebee; रमा (ramā) – Lakshmi; शम्भु (śambhu) – Shiva; ब्रह्मा (brahmā) – Brahma; अमरपति (amarapati) – Indra; गणेश (gaṇeśa) – Ganesha; अर्चित (arcita) – worshiped; पदः (padaḥ) – feet; जगन्नाथः (jagannāthaḥ) – Lord of the Universe; स्वामी (svāmī) – Lord; नयन (nayana) – eye; पथ (patha) – path; गामी (gāmī) – going; भवतु (bhavatu) – may be; मे (me) – my;

contentBlocks.original:
भुजे सव्ये वेणुं शिरसि शिखिपिच्छं कटितटे
दुकूलं नेत्रान्ते सहचर-कटाक्षं विदधते ।
सदा श्रीमद्‍-वृन्दावन-वसति-लीला-परिचयो
जगन्नाथः स्वामी नयन-पथ-गामी भवतु मे ॥२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhuje savye veṇuṃ śirasi śikhipicchaṃ kaṭitaṭe
dukūlaṃ netrānte sahacara-kaṭākṣaṃ vidadhate .
sadā śrīmad-vṛndāvana-vasati-līlā-paricayo
jagannāthaḥ svāmī nayana-patha-gāmī bhavatu me ..2..
contentBlocks.translation: In his left hand Lord Jagannatha holds a flute. On his head he wears the feathers of peacocks and on his hips he wears fine yellow silken cloth. Out of the corners of his eyes he bestows sidelong glances upon his loving devotees and he always reveals himself through his pastimes in his divine abode of Vrindavana. May that lord be the object of my vision.
contentBlocks.meanings: भुजे (bhuje) – in the arm; सव्ये (savye) – left; वेणुं (veṇuṃ) – flute; शिरसि (śirasi) – on the head; शिखिपिच्छं (śikhipicchaṃ) – peacock feathers (śikhi:peacock | picchaṃ:feathers); कटितटे (kaṭitaṭe) – on the hips; दुकूलं (dukūlaṃ) – silken cloth; नेत्रान्ते (netrānte) – corners of eyes (netra:eyes | ante:corners); सहचर-कटाक्षं (sahacara-kaṭākṣaṃ) – sidelong glances (sahacara:companion | kaṭākṣaṃ:glances); विदधते (vidadhate) – bestows; सदा (sadā) – always; श्रीमद्‍-वृन्दावन-वसति-लीला-परिचयो (śrīmad-vṛndāvana-vasati-līlā-paricayo) – known for pastimes in Vrindavana (śrīmad:glorious | vṛndāvana:Vrindavana | vasati:residence | līlā:pastimes | paricayaḥ:known for); जगन्नाथः (jagannāthaḥ) – Lord of the Universe (jagat:universe | nāthaḥ:lord); स्वामी (svāmī) – master; नयन-पथ-गामी (nayana-patha-gāmī) – object of vision (nayana:eye | patha:path | gāmī:going); भवतु (bhavatu) – may be; मे (me) – my;

contentBlocks.original:
महाम्भोधेस्तीरे कनक रुचिरे नील शिखरे
वसन् प्रासादान्तः सहज बलभद्रेण बलिना ।
सुभद्रा मध्यस्थः सकलसुर सेवावसरदो
जगन्नाथः स्वामी नयन-पथ-गामी भवतु मे ॥३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahāmbhodhestīre kanaka rucire nīla śikhare
vasan prāsādāntaḥ sahaja balabhadreṇa balinā .
subhadrā madhyasthaḥ sakalasura sevāvasarado
jagannāthaḥ svāmī nayana-patha-gāmī bhavatu me ..3..
contentBlocks.translation: Residing on the shore of the great ocean, within a large palace situated upon the crest of the brilliant, golden Nilacala Hill, along with his powerful brother Bala-bhadra, and in the middle of them his sister Subhadra, Lord Jagannatha bestows the opportunity for devotional service upon all godly souls. May that lord be the object of my vision.
contentBlocks.meanings: महाम्भोधेः (mahāmbhodheḥ) – of the great ocean (mahā:great | ambhodheḥ:of the ocean); तीरे (tīre) – on the shore; कनक (kanaka) – golden; रुचिरे (rucire) – brilliant; नील (nīla) – blue; शिखरे (śikhare) – on the crest; वसन् (vasan) – residing; प्रासादान्तः (prāsādāntaḥ) – within the palace (prāsāda:palace | antaḥ:within); सहज (sahaja) – natural; बलभद्रेण (balabhadreṇa) – with Balabhadra; बलिना (balinā) – with the powerful; सुभद्रा (subhadrā) – Subhadra; मध्यस्थः (madhyasthaḥ) – in the middle; सकलसुर (sakalasura) – all godly souls (sakala:all | sura:godly souls); सेवावसरदः (sevāvasaradaḥ) – bestows opportunity for service (sevā:service | avasaradaḥ:bestows opportunity); जगन्नाथः (jagannāthaḥ) – Lord Jagannatha; स्वामी (svāmī) – lord; नयन-पथ-गामी (nayana-patha-gāmī) – object of vision (nayana:eye | patha:path | gāmī:goer); भवतु (bhavatu) – may be; मे (me) – my;

contentBlocks.original:
कृपा पारावारः सजल जलद श्रेणिरुचिरो
रमा वाणी रामः स्फुरद् अमल पङ्केरुहमुखः ।
सुरेन्द्रैर् आराध्यः श्रुतिगण शिखा गीत चरितो
जगन्नाथः स्वामी नयन पथ गामी भवतु मे ॥४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kṛpā pārāvāraḥ sajala jalada śreṇiruciro
ramā vāṇī rāmaḥ sphurad amala paṅkeruhamukhaḥ .
surendrair ārādhyaḥ śrutigaṇa śikhā gīta carito
jagannāthaḥ svāmī nayana patha gāmī bhavatu me ..4..
contentBlocks.translation: Lord Jagannatha is an ocean of mercy and he is beautiful like a row of blackish rain clouds. he is the storehouse of bliss for Lakshmi and Saraswati, and his face is like a spotless full-blown lotus. He is worshiped by the best of demigods and sages, and his glories are sung by the Upanishads. May that lord be the object of my vision.
contentBlocks.meanings: कृपा (kṛpā) – mercy; पारावारः (pārāvāraḥ) – ocean; सजल (sajala) – with water; जलद (jalada) – cloud; श्रेणि (śreṇi) – row; रुचिरः (ruciraḥ) – beautiful; रमा (ramā) – Lakshmi; वाणी (vāṇī) – Saraswati; रामः (rāmaḥ) – storehouse of bliss; स्फुरत् (sphurat) – shining; अमल (amala) – spotless; पङ्केरुह (paṅkeruha) – lotus; मुखः (mukhaḥ) – face; सुरेन्द्रैः (surendraiḥ) – by the best of demigods; आराध्यः (ārādhyaḥ) – worshiped; श्रुतिगण (śrutigaṇa) – by the Upanishads (śruti:Upanishads | gaṇa:group); शिखा (śikhā) – peak; गीत (gīta) – sung; चरितः (caritaḥ) – glories; जगन्नाथः (jagannāthaḥ) – Lord Jagannatha; स्वामी (svāmī) – Lord; नयन (nayana) – eye; पथ (patha) – path; गामी (gāmī) – object of vision; भवतु (bhavatu) – may be; मे (me) – my;

contentBlocks.original:
रथारूढो गच्छन् पथि मिलित भूदेव पटलैः
स्तुति प्रादुर्भावम् प्रतिपदमुपाकर्ण्य सदयः ।
दया सिन्धुर्बन्धुः सकल जगतां सिन्धु सुतया
जगन्नाथः स्वामी नयन पथ गामी भवतु मे ॥५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rathārūḍho gacchan pathi milita bhūdeva paṭalaiḥ
stuti prādurbhāvam pratipadamupākarṇya sadayaḥ .
dayā sindhurbandhuḥ sakala jagatāṃ sindhu sutayā
jagannāthaḥ svāmī nayana patha gāmī bhavatu me ..5..
contentBlocks.translation: When Lord Jagannatha is on his Ratha-yatra cart and is moving along the road, at every step there is prayers and songs chanted by large assemblies of brahmanas. hearing their hymns Lord Jagannatha is very favorably disposed towards them. He is the ocean of mercy and the true friend of all the worlds. May that lord, along with his consort Lakshmi, who was born from the ocean of nectar, be the object of my vision.
contentBlocks.meanings: रथारूढः (rathārūḍhaḥ) – mounted on the chariot (ratha:chariot | ārūḍhaḥ:mounted); गच्छन् (gacchan) – going; पथि (pathi) – on the path; मिलित (milita) – assembled; भूदेव (bhūdeva) – brahmanas (bhū:earth | deva:god); पटलैः (paṭalaiḥ) – by groups; स्तुति (stuti) – praise; प्रादुर्भावम् (prādurbhāvam) – appearance; प्रतिपदम् (pratipadam) – at every step; उपाकर्ण्य (upākarṇya) – hearing; सदयः (sadayaḥ) – compassionate; दया (dayā) – mercy; सिन्धुः (sindhuḥ) – ocean; बन्धुः (bandhuḥ) – friend; सकल (sakala) – all; जगताम् (jagatām) – of the worlds; सिन्धु (sindhu) – ocean; सुतया (sutayā) – with the daughter; जगन्नाथः (jagannāthaḥ) – Lord of the Universe (jagat:universe | nāthaḥ:lord); स्वामी (svāmī) – master; नयन (nayana) – eye; पथ (patha) – path; गामी (gāmī) – going; भवतु (bhavatu) – may be; मे (me) – my;

contentBlocks.original:
परंब्रह्मापीड़ः कुवलय-दलोत्‍फुल्ल-नयनो
निवासी नीलाद्रौ निहित-चरणोऽनन्त-शिरसि ।
रसानन्दी राधा-सरस-वपुरालिङ्गन-सुखो
जगन्नाथः स्वामी नयन-पथगामी भवतु मे ॥६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
parambrahmāpīr̤aḥ kuvalaya-dalotphulla-nayano
nivāsī nīlādrau nihita-caraṇo'nanta-śirasi .
rasānandī rādhā-sarasa-vapurāliṅgana-sukho
jagannāthaḥ svāmī nayana-pathagāmī bhavatu me ..6..
contentBlocks.translation: He is the ornament of the head of Lord Brahma and his eyes are like the full-blown petals of the lotus. He resides on the Nilacala Hill, and his lotus feet are placed on the heads of Ananta Deva. Lord Jagannatha is overwhelmed by the mellow of love and he becomes joyful in the embracing of the body of Sri Radharani, which is like a cool pond. May that lord be the object of my vision.
contentBlocks.meanings: परंब्रह्मापीड़ः (parambrahmāpīḍaḥ) – ornament of Brahma's head (parama:supreme | brahma:Brahma | āpīḍaḥ:ornament of head); कुवलय-दलोत्फुल्ल-नयनो (kuvalaya-dalotphulla-nayano) – eyes like lotus petals (kuvalaya:lotus | dala:petal | utphulla:fully bloomed | nayano:eyes); निवासी (nivāsī) – resident; नीलाद्रौ (nīlādrau) – on the Nilacala Hill (nīla:blue | adrau:on the mountain); निहित-चरणः (nihita-caraṇaḥ) – feet placed (nihita:placed | caraṇaḥ:feet); अनन्त-शिरसि (ananta-śirasi) – on Ananta's head (ananta:Ananta | śirasi:on the head); रसानन्दी (rasānandī) – overwhelmed by love (rasa:mellow | ānandī:joyful); राधा-सरस-वपुरालिङ्गन-सुखः (rādhā-sarasa-vapurāliṅgana-sukhaḥ) – joyful in Radha's embrace (rādhā:Radha | sarasa:cool pond | vapuḥ:body | āliṅgana:embrace | sukhaḥ:joyful); जगन्नाथः (jagannāthaḥ) – Lord Jagannatha; स्वामी (svāmī) – lord; नयन-पथगामी (nayana-pathagāmī) – object of vision (nayana:eye | patha:path | gāmī:going); भवतु (bhavatu) – may be; मे (me) – my;

contentBlocks.original:
न वै याचे राज्यं न च कनक माणिक्य विभवं
न याचेऽहं रम्यां सकल जन काम्यां वरवधूम् ।
सदा काले काले प्रमथ पतिना गीतचरितो
जगन्नाथः स्वामी नयन पथ गामी भवतु मे ॥७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
na vai yāce rājyaṃ na ca kanaka māṇikya vibhavaṃ
na yāce'haṃ ramyāṃ sakala jana kāmyāṃ varavadhūm .
sadā kāle kāle pramatha patinā gītacarito
jagannāthaḥ svāmī nayana patha gāmī bhavatu me ..7..
contentBlocks.translation: I do not pray for a kingdom, nor for gold, rubies, and wealth. I do not ask for an excellent and beautiful wife as desired by all men. I simply pray that lord, whose glories are always sung by Lord Siva, be the constant object of my vision.
contentBlocks.meanings: न (na) – not; वै (vai) – indeed; याचे (yāce) – I pray; राज्यं (rājyaṃ) – kingdom; न (na) – not; च (ca) – and; कनक (kanaka) – gold; माणिक्य (māṇikya) – rubies; विभवं (vibhavaṃ) – wealth; न (na) – not; याचेऽहं (yāce'haṃ) – I ask; रम्यां (ramyāṃ) – beautiful; सकल (sakala) – all; जन (jana) – people; काम्यां (kāmyāṃ) – desired; वरवधूम् (varavadhūm) – excellent wife; सदा (sadā) – always; काले (kāle) – time; काले (kāle) – time; प्रमथ (pramatha) – Lord Śiva; पतिना (patinā) – by the lord; गीतचरितो (gītacarito) – whose glories are sung (gīta:sung | caritaḥ:glories); जगन्नाथः (jagannāthaḥ) – Lord of the Universe (jagat:universe | nāthaḥ:lord); स्वामी (svāmī) – master; नयन (nayana) – eye; पथ (patha) – path; गामी (gāmī) – goer; भवतु (bhavatu) – may be; मे (me) – my;

contentBlocks.original:
हर त्वं संसारं द्रुततरम् असारं सुरपते
हर त्वं पापानां विततिम् अपरां यादवपते ।
अहो दीनेऽनाथे निहित चरणो निश्चितमिदं
जगन्नाथः स्वामी नयन पथ गामी भवतु मे ॥८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hara tvaṃ saṃsāraṃ drutataram asāraṃ surapate
hara tvaṃ pāpānāṃ vitatim aparāṃ yādavapate .
aho dīne'nāthe nihita caraṇo niścitamidaṃ
jagannāthaḥ svāmī nayana patha gāmī bhavatu me ..8..
contentBlocks.translation: O Lord of the demigods, please quickly remove this useless material existence I am undergoing. O Lord of the Yadus, please destroy this vast ocean of sins which has no shore. Alas, this is certain that Lord Jagannatha’s lotus feet are bestowed upon those who feel themselves fallen and have no shelter in this world but Him. May that lord be the object of my vision.
contentBlocks.meanings: हर (hara) – remove; त्वं (tvaṃ) – you; संसारं (saṃsāraṃ) – material existence; द्रुततरम् (drutataram) – quickly; असारं (asāraṃ) – useless; सुरपते (surapate) – O Lord of the demigods (sura:demigods | pate:O Lord); हर (hara) – destroy; त्वं (tvaṃ) – you; पापानां (pāpānāṃ) – of sins; विततिम् (vitatim) – vast ocean; अपरां (aparāṃ) – which has no shore; यादवपते (yādavapate) – O Lord of the Yadus (yādava:Yadus | pate:O Lord); अहो (aho) – alas; दीनेऽनाथे (dīne'nāthe) – fallen and shelterless (dīne:fallen | anāthe:shelterless); निहित (nihita) – bestowed; चरणो (caraṇo) – feet; निश्चितमिदं (niścitamidaṃ) – this is certain (niścitam:certain | idaṃ:this); जगन्नाथः (jagannāthaḥ) – Lord Jagannatha (jagat:world | nāthaḥ:Lord); स्वामी (svāmī) – Lord; नयन (nayana) – vision; पथ (patha) – path; गामी (gāmī) – object; भवतु (bhavatu) – may be; मे (me) – my;

contentBlocks.original:
इति श्रीमद् शङ्कराचार्यप्रणीतं जगन्नाथाष्टकं सम्पूर्णं॥
जगन्नाथाष्टकं पुन्यं यः पठेत् प्रयतः शुचिः ।
सर्वपाप विशुद्धात्मा विष्णुलोकं स गच्छति ॥९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
iti śrīmad śaṅkarācāryapraṇītaṃ jagannāthāṣṭakaṃ sampūrṇaṃ..
jagannāthāṣṭakaṃ punyaṃ yaḥ paṭhet prayataḥ śuciḥ .
sarvapāpa viśuddhātmā viṣṇulokaṃ sa gacchati ..9..
contentBlocks.translation:
This is the completion of the Jagannatha Ashtakam by Srimad Shankaracharya.
The self-retrained, virtuous soul who recites these eight verses glorifying Lord Jagannatha becomes cleansed of all sins and duly proceeds to Lord Visnu's abode.
contentBlocks.meanings: इति (iti) – thus; श्रीमद् (śrīmad) – glorious; शङ्कराचार्यप्रणीतं (śaṅkarācāryapraṇītaṃ) – composed by Shankaracharya (śaṅkara:Shankara | ācārya:teacher | praṇītaṃ:composed); जगन्नाथाष्टकं (jagannāthāṣṭakaṃ) – eight verses on Jagannatha (jagannātha:Lord of the Universe | aṣṭakaṃ:eight verses); सम्पूर्णं (sampūrṇaṃ) – complete; जगन्नाथाष्टकं (jagannāthāṣṭakaṃ) – eight verses on Jagannatha (jagannātha:Lord of the Universe | aṣṭakaṃ:eight verses); पुन्यं (puṇyaṃ) – auspicious; यः (yaḥ) – who; पठेत् (paṭhet) – recites; प्रयतः (prayataḥ) – self-restrained; शुचिः (śuciḥ) – pure; सर्वपाप (sarvapāpa) – all sins (sarva:all | pāpa:sins); विशुद्धात्मा (viśuddhātmā) – purified soul (viśuddha:purified | ātmā:soul); विष्णुलोकं (viṣṇulokaṃ) – abode of Vishnu (viṣṇu:Vishnu | lokaṃ:abode); स (sa) – he; गच्छति (gacchati) – goes;
